title: IMQ Increment 9 – Experience Master Requirements Seed Set
body:
1. Business Context and Justification

IMQ exists to provide objective, auditable evidence that a worker is qualified to perform assigned tasks within regulated life sciences environments.

Qualification defensibility requires structured evaluation of:

Education

Experience

Training

Certifications

Increment 8 introduced a structured Education Master Requirements dataset.

Increment 9 introduces a structured Experience Master Requirements dataset.

The purpose of this increment is to:

Standardize experience expectations across roles

Enable consistent Job Requirement composition

Improve audit defensibility

Expand Master Requirements List (MRL) coverage

Prepare the system for future AI-assisted experience duration interpretation

This increment does NOT introduce AI duration parsing, CV semantic analysis, or probabilistic scoring. It standardizes experience requirement language only.

2. Architectural Intent

This increment expands the existing Master Requirements List.

It must:

Append 40 Experience-type requirements

Preserve all existing Education requirements

Preserve all existing Training requirements

Preserve existing API contracts

Preserve existing domain model

Preserve Qualification Engine logic

Introduce no schema changes

This is a data-only expansion.

No refactoring is permitted in this increment.

3. Functional Behavior

After implementation:

Managers must be able to:

Select Experience requirements when building a Job Requirement

Filter Master Requirements by RequirementType = Experience

Filter by Approval Status

View Experience requirements in the Master Requirements screen

The system must:

Maintain deterministic filtering

Maintain tenant isolation

Maintain clean build integrity

Preserve existing Education and Training behavior

4. Data Model Constraints

Use existing entity:

QualificationRequirement

Use existing enum values only:

QualificationRequirementType.Experience
RequirementLevel.Required
RequirementLevel.Preferred
RequirementApprovalStatus.Approved
RequirementApprovalStatus.Draft
RequirementApprovalStatus.Obsolete

No new properties introduced.
No entity refactoring.
No new relationships introduced.

5. Acceptance Criteria

AC1: Exactly 40 Experience records added
AC2: All records have RequirementType = Experience
AC3: No null required fields

AC4: Majority Approved
AC5: Exactly 4 Draft
AC6: Exactly 2 Obsolete

AC7: Filtering by RequirementType = Experience returns exactly 40 records
AC8: Filtering by RequirementType = Education remains unchanged
AC9: Filtering by RequirementType = Training remains unchanged

AC10: Approval status filtering deterministic

AC11: No API contract changes
AC12: No new endpoints

AC13: No compilation warnings
AC14: All existing tests pass

6. Authoritative Seed Payload (Do Not Modify)

Append exactly the following records to the existing MRL seed provider.

Do not modify Education records.
Do not refactor domain model.
Do not introduce new properties.

7. Documentation Generation Requirement

As part of this increment:

Generate or update documentation artifacts to reflect:

Business rationale for Experience MRL

Updated functional behavior

Updated master requirement coverage

Traceability alignment

Generate clean, structured:

User Requirements update section

Functional Requirements update section

Traceability references

Documentation must reflect that this increment:

Expands master requirement coverage

Introduces no architectural changes

Introduces no engine logic changes
